messages: Quick gardening question: when's the best time to prune lavender in cooler climates?
By the way, did you know octopuses have three hearts? Nature never stops surprising me.

Okay, back to the language plan — given the timeframe I mentioned earlier, would focusing most study on short daily spaced-repetition sessions plus a weekly conversation slot realistically get me to an intermediate-ish level?
If time gets tight, would you cut down listening practice or reduce flashcard reps first to keep progress steady?
When push comes to shove, should I emphasize character recognition or active vocabulary in the month leading up to the test for the biggest score gains?
Finally, in the final stretch before the exam, is it sensible to switch to more mock tests and focused speaking practice, or is that overkill?
